qtype: cost calculation, ratio and proportion, percentage of total, profit calculation
tex:
A grocer sells potatoes, mushrooms and carrots.
\newline

(a) A customer buys 3 kg of mushrooms at \$1.04 per kg and 4 kg of carrots at \$1.28 per kg.
\newline

Calculate the total cost.
\newline

(b) In one week, the ratio of the masses of vegetables sold by the grocer is

\centerline{potatoes : mushrooms : carrots = 11 : 8 : 6.
\newline
}

(i) (c) Work out the mass of mushrooms sold as a percentage of the total mass.
\newline

(d) The total mass of potatoes, mushrooms and carrots sold is 1500 kg.
\newline

Find the mass of carrots the grocer sells this week.
\newline

(e) The profit the grocer makes selling 1 kg of carrots is \$0.75 .
\newline

Find the total profit the grocer makes selling carrots this week.
\newline

(f) On the last day of the week, the grocer reduces the price of 1 kg of potatoes by 8\% to \$1.15 .
\newline

Calculate the original price of 1 kg of potatoes.
\newline

(g) The grocer buys 620 kg of onions, correct to the nearest 20 kg.
\newline
He packs them into bags each containing 5 kg of onions, correct to the nearest 1 kg.
\newline

Calculate the upper bound for the number of bags of onions that he packs.
\newline

content.content:
A customer buys 3 kg of mushrooms at \$1.04 per kg and 4 kg of carrots at \$1.28 per kg.
\newline

Calculate the total cost.
\newline

In one week, the ratio of the masses of vegetables sold by the grocer is

\centerline{potatoes : mushrooms : carrots = 11 : 8 : 6.
\newline
}

Work out the mass of mushrooms sold as a percentage of the total mass.
\newline

The total mass of potatoes, mushrooms and carrots sold is 1500 kg.
\newline

Find the mass of carrots the grocer sells this week.
\newline

The profit the grocer makes selling 1 kg of carrots is \$0.75 .
\newline

Find the total profit the grocer makes selling carrots this week.
\newline

On the last day of the week, the grocer reduces the price of 1 kg of potatoes by 8\% to \$1.15 .
\newline

Calculate the original price of 1 kg of potatoes.
\newline

The grocer buys 620 kg of onions, correct to the nearest 20 kg.
\newline
He packs them into bags each containing 5 kg of onions, correct to the nearest 1 kg.
\newline

Calculate the upper bound for the number of bags of onions that he packs.
\newline

stem.content:
A grocer sells potatoes, mushrooms and carrots.
\newline

\newline
]

qtype: factorisation of quadratics, simplifying algebraic fractions, expanding and simplifying algebraic expressions, solving simultaneous linear and non-linear equations
tex:
(a) (i) (b) Factorise.
\newline
$$x^2 - x - 12$$

(c) Simplify.
\newline
$$\frac{x^2 - 16}{x^2 - x - 12}$$

(d) Simplify.
\newline
$$(2x - 3)^2 - (x + 1)^2$$

(e) Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline
$$\frac{2x + 4}{x + 1} - \frac{x}{x - 3}$$

(f) Expand and simplify.
\newline
$$(x - 3) (x - 5) (2x + 1)$$

(g) Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline
You must show all your working.
\newline

\begin{align*}
x - 3y &= 13 \\
2x^2 - 9y &= 116
\end{align*}

content.content:
Factorise.
\newline
$$x^2 - x - 12$$

Simplify.
\newline
$$\frac{x^2 - 16}{x^2 - x - 12}$$

Simplify.
\newline
$$(2x - 3)^2 - (x + 1)^2$$

Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline
$$\frac{2x + 4}{x + 1} - \frac{x}{x - 3}$$

Expand and simplify.
\newline
$$(x - 3) (x - 5) (2x + 1)$$

Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline
You must show all your working.
\newline

\begin{align*}
x - 3y &= 13 \\
2x^2 - 9y &= 116
\end{align*}

qtype: simple interest, compound interest, exponential growth
tex:
(a) Janna and Kamal each invest \$8000.
\newline
At the end of 12 years, they each have \$$12\,800$.
\newline

(i) (b) Janna invests in an account that pays simple interest at a rate of $r$\% per year.
\newline

Calculate the value of $r$.
\newline

(c) Kamal invests in an account that pays compound interest at a rate of $R$\% per year.
\newline

Calculate the value of $R$.
\newline

(d) The population of a city is growing exponentially at a rate of 1.8\% per year.
\newline
The population now is $260\,000$.
\newline

Find the number of complete years from now when the population will first be more than $300\,000$.
\newline

content.content:

\newline

\newline

\newline

Calculate the value of $r$.
\newline

Kamal invests in an account that pays compound interest at a rate of $R$\% per year.
\newline

Calculate the value of $R$.
\newline

The population of a city is growing exponentially at a rate of 1.8\% per year.
\newline
The population now is $260\,000$.
\newline

qtype: composite functions, inverse functions, function substitution, solving equations involving functions (including exponential)
tex:

(a) Find.
\newline

(c) $g^{-1}(x)$

(d) Find in its simplest form $g(x - 2)$.
\newline

(e) Find the value of $x$ when

(i) (f) $fg(x) = 0.1$

(g) $h(x) - g(7) = 0$.
\newline

content.content:
Find.
\newline

Find in its simplest form $g(x - 2)$.
\newline
Find the value of $x$ when

$h(x) - g(7) = 0$.
